accurate.why: All claims match the bill text (section 547(f), figures, fiscal years) and the program context (BCOR under PHSA) is verified.

no_hallucinations.why: Program name and PHSA context are accurate; no fabricated claims.

states_purpose.why: Clearly states the bill reauthorizes funding for the Building Communities of Recovery grant program.

changes_to_existing_law.why: Correctly identifies the amendment to section 547(f) of the Public Health Service Act and the change in authorization.

exceptions_conditions.why: The bill contains no exceptions or carve-outs beyond the funding change.

effective_dates.why: Correctly notes the fiscal years 2026-2030 and that no separate effective date is specified.

major_provisions_covered.why: Captures the bill's sole operative effect: replacing the prior authorization with $17,000,000 for FY2026-2030.

objective_tone.why: The summary is neutral and free of editorializing.

coherent.why: The summary is well-organized and internally consistent.

concise.why: The summary is appropriately brief without padding.

correct_entities.why: Public Health Service Act, section 547(f), and the Building Communities of Recovery program are all correctly identified.

correct_figures.why: The figures ($5,000,000 FY2019-2023; $17,000,000 FY2026-2030) match the bill text exactly.
